items.title: Empowering Android with Computer Use Features in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Computer Use capability to Android, allowing on-device automation through a native AccessibilityService plugin. Key features include gesture control, typing, and screenshot capabilities, all integrated into the chat loop for seamless interaction. The implementation ensures that tools are only advertised when the accessibility service is enabled, maintaining user consent and security.

items.title: Enhance @-mentions for text sends in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces real @-mentions in text sends, allowing notifications for specific members and the option to mention all members in a group. The `send_text` function now accepts a list of mentions and a boolean for mentioning all, improving communication efficiency. Testing has confirmed successful functionality in live environments.

items.title: New Android Computer Use Plan in Index 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new plan for Android that mirrors the desktop Computer Use functionality. This includes features like screenshot capture and gesture control using AccessibilityService and MediaProjection. The implementation will be phased, starting with a native plugin and progressing through various stages to enhance user experience while adhering to Play policy restrictions.

items.title: Center Sidebar Logo for a Sleeker Look in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update fixes the alignment of the AceData logo in the console sidebar, ensuring it is centered rather than pinned to the left. The changes involve modifying the CSS and template structure to allow for proper flexbox alignment, enhancing the visual consistency across the application. Additionally, the height of the logo container is now properly defined, preventing collapse issues.

items.title: Enhance Seedance Prompt Control in Platform Service 🎬
items.summary: This update introduces the official Seedance prompt specification and subtitle mitigation strategies to improve the short-drama dialogue clips. Key changes include the addition of a no-subtitle instruction and a refined prompt structure to ensure accurate dialogue generation. The version has been updated from 1.8.1 to 1.9.0, with modifications made to both SKILL.md and data-contract.md files.

items.title: Streamline Screenshot Handling in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the `computer.screenshot` functionality to upload images as hosted URLs instead of inline base64 data URIs. This change reduces storage and bandwidth usage while preventing issues with conversation context eviction. The implementation ensures that screenshots are treated like regular image attachments, enhancing consistency across the platform.

items.title: Clean up dead code in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `protectHtmlPreBlocks` and `restoreHtmlPreBlocks` functions from the VueMarkdown component, simplifying the rendering process. With the introduction of a new rendering method that bypasses markdown-it for the service introduction page, this shim is now obsolete. The changes ensure that Markdown documents with embedded `<pre>` tags are rendered correctly without any regressions.

items.title: Fix HTML Rendering for Service Intro Pages in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the rendering of service introduction pages by bypassing markdown-it for raw HTML content. A new `detectHtml` switch has been added to the MarkdownRenderer, allowing full HTML documents to be rendered verbatim, eliminating the risk of leaked tags and formatting errors. This change ensures that all service introduction pages are displayed correctly across all languages and future additions.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI CLI with API-Backed Models Command in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the OpenAI CLI with the new API specification by replacing the static model listing with a dynamic call to the `/openai/models` endpoint. The `openai-cli models` command now supports JSON output and includes enhancements to the `OpenAIClient` for better request handling. Additionally, documentation and tests have been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring a smooth user experience.

items.title: Add OpenAPI definition for OpenAI Models API in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the missing OpenAPI 3.0 definition for the OpenAI Models API, resolving an issue where the API card displayed empty badges. The new definition includes a complete `GET` operation with security details, response schemas, and translations, ensuring the API is fully functional and properly documented. This change enhances the user experience by providing accurate endpoint information and improves the overall API consistency.

items.title: Enhance Zhihu Skill with Search Functionality in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new search capability to the Zhihu skill by adding a standalone CLI script that utilizes the Zhihu Developer Platform API. Users can now search Zhihu content, explore global web results, and view trending topics without needing login cookies. The implementation ensures a clean, zero-dependency approach, enhancing the overall functionality of the Zhihu skill.

items.title: Streamline Platform Environment Injection in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the platform environment injection by replacing hardcoded provider checks with a generic call to the new AuthBackend endpoint. Key changes include the addition of a `getPlatformEnv()` method and the removal of specific Google Ads configurations, simplifying the process of adding new platform secrets. This enhancement allows for easier integration of future providers without modifying worker code.

items.title: Add platform-env endpoint for secret injection in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new internal endpoint, `GET /internal/v1/connectors/platform-env/`, which resolves platform-held environment variables for connectors. It allows the aichat2 worker to inject secrets like `ZHIHU_DEVELOPER_TOKEN` and `GOOGLE_ADS_DEVELOPER_TOKEN` alongside user credentials. Key changes include the addition of a new view and route, as well as updates to the connectors configuration.

items.title: Enhance CLI Parameters for Dreamina and WebExtrator in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the Dreamina and WebExtrator CLI parameters with the latest OpenAPI documentation. Key changes include making the task ID optional in Dreamina and adding new options for resource blocking and header parsing in WebExtrator. Additionally, focused test updates ensure the new functionalities are thoroughly validated.

items.title: Revamp API Pricing Table for Better Readability in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update centers and compacts the API pricing table for improved visual balance. Key changes include centering all content, constraining the table width, and removing the triple strikethrough for original prices, which significantly reduces row height. The result is a cleaner, more user-friendly pricing display without altering any pricing data.

items.title: Localize API Summaries for Enhanced Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update localizes 18 API operation summaries by replacing hardcoded English text with dynamic translation references. The changes ensure that the API documentation reflects localized descriptions based on user language preferences, enhancing accessibility for diverse users. All modifications have been validated and are already visible in production.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Resilience After Pod Restarts in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses cascading 401 errors caused by pod restarts that wiped in-memory OAuth state. Key changes include preventing refresh tokens from being issued, synthesizing permissive stubs for unknown client IDs, and allowing graceful handling of unknown refresh tokens. These improvements ensure zero downtime for clients with valid Bearer tokens, enhancing overall system reliability.

items.title: New per-tool toggles for built-in local tools in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces individual 'always allow' toggles for built-in local tools, enhancing user control over permissions. Users can now pre-approve tools like `shell.run_command` and file system operations, streamlining their experience while maintaining security through native confirmation dialogs. The changes also include updates to internationalization across 18 locales.

items.title: Restore Google Drive Broad Scopes in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the broad Google Drive OAuth scopes, `drive` and `drive.readonly`, on the `google/drive` connector, reversing a previous restriction. Key documentation and test assertions have been updated accordingly to reflect these changes. This enhancement allows for a full read/write experience with Google Drive, improving user functionality.

items.title: Fix empty redirect_uris issue in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue in the aichat2 worker where an empty `redirect_uris` caused confusion during token expiration handling. Key changes include ensuring `clientInformation()` returns a valid stub and providing a proper `redirectUrl` to facilitate user re-authorization. These adjustments enhance error clarity and maintain healthy connection functionality.

items.title: Housekeeping Updates and New Plans in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update includes the addition of new plan documents in the `plans/` directory, covering various topics such as Google One Tap SSO and Maestro standalone site. Additionally, the `.gitignore` file has been modified to exclude sensitive Apple keys, and the local MCP configuration file has been removed to streamline the repository. These changes enhance documentation clarity and improve repository hygiene.

items.title: Refresh Featured Models and Media Configuration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refreshes the featured models on the platform to include the latest flagships from various providers, ensuring users see the most current options. Additionally, media models can now be featured through a new configuration file, allowing for easier updates without code changes. This enhances the flexibility and maintainability of the catalog system.

items.title: Empower Computer Use with Individual Toggles in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces individual always-allow toggles for various computer actions, enhancing user control over AI interactions. Users can now pre-approve specific actions like screenshotting or typing, rather than granting blanket permissions. The feature maintains security by requiring confirmation for each action and allows for easy revocation.

items.title: Update i18n Rules for Intro Page Generation in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the Two-File Convention by correcting outdated references and introducing new i18n rules. Non-zh-CN versions must now replicate the `<style>` and `<pre>` sections from the zh-CN version verbatim, ensuring consistency across translations. These changes aim to prevent issues with machine translation in future page generations.

items.title: Enhance i18n Guidelines for Introduction Page in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update adds crucial guidelines to the introduction design documentation, specifically addressing the pitfalls of machine translation for multi-language support. It emphasizes that introduction pages must not be fully machine-translated to prevent formatting issues, ensuring that code and styles are preserved accurately across different locales. The changes aim to enhance the quality and consistency of localized content.

items.title: Introducing Claude AI CLI for Enhanced Interaction in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new `claude/` package, providing a command-line interface for interacting with Claude AI models via AceDataCloud. Key commands include `claude chat`, `claude messages`, and `claude count-tokens`, allowing users to leverage both OpenAI-compatible and native Claude APIs. The addition of 18 models enhances flexibility and usability for developers.

items.title: Revamp Pricing Table for Clarity in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: The Pricing tab has been significantly improved for better readability and visual hierarchy. Key changes include removing excessive bold styling, eliminating redundant discount badges, and restructuring the Actual Price cell for a cleaner layout. These adjustments enhance user experience without altering any pricing logic or data.

items.title: Fix MT Code Blocks in Locale Intro Pages in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses issues with machine-translated code blocks in various language introduction pages. Key changes include restoring code indentation, removing incorrect code fences, and ensuring that the original translations remain intact. A total of 477 files were modified to enhance the readability and functionality of the documentation across multiple languages.

items.title: Fix API Method Badge Display in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the Gemini Generate Content API did not display the correct method badge due to a mismatch in path syntax. The solution involves prioritizing the backfilled `Api.method` field for determining the API method, ensuring that badges are displayed correctly even when using Kong `:param` syntax. New tests have been added to verify the functionality and ensure no regressions occur.

items.title: Enhance Messages API with Extended Thinking in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `thinking` parameter and associated response content blocks to the Claude Messages API OpenAPI specification. The changes ensure alignment with Anthropic's official API, enhancing the API reference and playground capabilities. Additionally, new translations have been added to support various locales, improving accessibility for users.

items.title: Pause Hourly Connector E2E CronJob in Backend ⏸️
items.summary: The hourly connector E2E smoke test has been temporarily suspended by setting `spec.suspend: true` in the CronJob manifest. This change ensures that no new runs are scheduled, clarifying the desired state and preventing any unintended reactivation during deployments. To re-enable the test, simply change the value back to `false` or remove the line.

items.title: Enhance message retrieval by using app DB for real-time updates in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of stale message results by prioritizing the app database for retrieving messages in group and contact chats. The new method ensures that live conversations are accurately reflected, falling back to the decrypted WeChat database only when necessary. Additionally, a new function `list_messages_by_conversation_name()` has been introduced to streamline message retrieval based on conversation names.

items.title: Add Claude-Sonnet-5 Pricing to Services in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `claude-sonnet-5` model to the pricing pages of five Claude services, ensuring it is billable and callable. Each service's pricing block is a direct clone of the existing `claude-sonnet-4-6` block, maintaining consistent pricing. The changes have been verified with successful tests, confirming the integrity of the pricing structure.

items.title: Enhance Feature Stripping for Upstream Accounts in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a configurable approach for stripping unsupported features in the Claude Messages API worker, allowing each upstream account to specify which features to remove. The changes ensure that capable upstreams can now pass through the 'thinking' parameter, enhancing functionality and preventing unnecessary errors. The implementation includes updates to the MongoDB accounts schema and various handler files, ensuring robust handling of unsupported features.

items.title: Shrink Org Logo for a Sleeker Look in .github 🎨
items.summary: This update reduces the width of the organization profile logo from 300px to 220px, ensuring a more compact appearance while maintaining high resolution. The change enhances the overall aesthetics of the README without altering any functionality.

items.title: Enhance MCP Servers Table with URL Column in .github 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new URL column to the MCP Servers table in the organization profile README. Each server's hosted Streamable-HTTP endpoint is now displayed alongside its GitHub repo and PyPI badge, improving accessibility and usability. The changes ensure that all 21 servers have valid URLs, with a scheduled workflow to maintain synchronization in future updates.

items.title: Optimize org logo size in .github 🎨
items.summary: The logo on the GitHub organization profile has been resized for better display. By switching from a Markdown image to an HTML `` tag with a specified width, the logo remains high-resolution while appearing smaller and more visually appealing.

items.title: Fix chat targeting to ensure messages go to the right recipient in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue where WeChat messages could be sent to the wrong recipient due to stale chat windows. Key changes include implementing strict title matching for opened chats and enhancing the search functionality to retry and clear previous searches. The behavior now requires exact matches, preventing mis-sends and improving overall reliability.

items.title: Reconnect Stale DB Connections for Smooth Sync in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new function, `ensure_db_connection()`, to handle stale database connections during long-running sync operations. By pinging the database before processing each file, we prevent silent errors that could lead to dropped records. This change enhances reliability without altering behavior on healthy connections.

items.title: Added July 2026 Creator-Fee Buyback in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update incorporates the on-chain creator-fee buyback executed on July 1, 2026, into the buyback history. The transaction involved 37.69 USDC, resulting in the purchase of 78,441 ACE through the DFlow Aggregator v4. All data has been verified for accuracy and is now available for transparency.

items.title: Update Model Allowlists with New OpenAPI Docs in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the CLI allowlists for AiChat, GLM, and OpenAI models with the latest OpenAPI documentation. New models such as `gpt-5.4-mini`, `gpt-5.4-nano`, `glm-5`, and `glm-5-turbo` have been added, enhancing command option validation and model listings. The changes ensure that users can access the most current model options seamlessly.

items.title: Exciting New Models Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several new chat models to the public /models directory, including Claude Sonnet 5, GPT-5.4-mini, GPT-5.4-nano, GLM-5, and DeepSeek-V4-Flash. The models have been benchmarked and integrated with existing billing rules, ensuring a seamless experience for users. All changes have been verified with 856 passing tests, confirming the integrity of the updates.

items.title: Updated Featured Models for Current Flagships in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The Featured badge on the models directory has been refreshed to showcase the latest flagship chat models. Key updates include the addition of GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Grok 4, ensuring that only one current flagship per major provider is highlighted. This change enhances the user experience by providing up-to-date recommendations.

items.title: Sync model names to match OpenAPI spec in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the CLI with the latest AceDataCloud OpenAPI spec by removing the `-preview` suffix from two chat model identifiers. Key changes include updates to the `GEMINI_CHAT_MODELS` list, the display table in `output.py`, and the corresponding tests in `test_commands.py`. The README has also been revised to reflect these changes, ensuring consistency across documentation and functionality.

items.title: Fix task history retrieval issue in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a bug where the `retrieve_batch` function returned a count greater than zero but an empty items array for many services. The root cause was identified as undefined keys being passed to MongoDB queries, which led to incorrect filtering. The fix involves stripping undefined keys before executing the query, ensuring accurate task history retrieval across ten affected workers.

items.title: Enhance Group Management Features in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the robustness of the WeChat group creation and invitation APIs, addressing issues found during live testing. Key changes include enhanced member selection verification, support for 微信号 input, and a new confirmation dialog for large group invitations. These enhancements ensure a smoother user experience and prevent silent errors during group management.

items.title: Transform Gemini Image Model for Compatibility in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a transformation for the official Gemini image model name, changing `gemini-3-pro-image` to `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`. This change ensures compatibility with upstream services and prevents mismatches in account matching and URL usage. The implementation has been verified with successful `py_compile` and `ruff` checks.

items.title: Expose Official Image Model IDs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the exposed image model IDs with Google's official Gemini API by removing the '-preview' suffix for external use. Key changes include modifications to the `_chat_models.json` for billing rules and updates to the `visible_models` interface to only show official names. Compatibility with the previous '-preview' naming is maintained internally, ensuring seamless transitions for existing users.

items.title: Update favicon for UnifiedHub center node in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The center node of the UnifiedHub has been updated to use the compact single-A favicon instead of the full wordmark logo. This change ensures consistency with the surrounding provider icons while maintaining a fallback to the packaged favicon for tenants without a configured one. The update is a simple one-line modification with no impact on logic or billing.

items.title: Align ephone 2.0 Default Resolution to Official Standard in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update corrects the default resolution for the ephone Seedance 2.0 model to 720p, aligning it with the official Volcengine ARK API specifications. Previously, the system defaulted to 1080p for certain models, which has now been fixed to ensure consistent billing and rendering. The change maintains support for explicit resolution requests while ensuring compliance with the official guidelines.

items.title: Exciting Updates: New Chat Models and Options in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces five new chat models and two new request parameters to the CLI, enhancing functionality and user experience. The new models include 'gemini-2.5-flash-lite' and 'gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview', among others. Additionally, the CLI now supports updated options for maximum completion tokens and service tier selection, streamlining command usage.

items.title: Enhance Gemini Image Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new branch in the image processing logic to ensure that `gemini-2.5-flash-image` is correctly passed through without being downgraded to a text model. The change prevents issues with account matching that could lead to incorrect outputs. All relevant tests have passed successfully.

items.title: Enhance Gemini Image Models and Fix Pricing Drift in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new image models, `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, with respective pricing of $0.14 and $0.35 per image. Additionally, it addresses a pricing drift issue by ensuring accurate cost calculations and updating the service display prices. The changes have been thoroughly tested and are ready for deployment.

items.title: Enhance MCP Tools with Backend Filters in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates previously unsupported backend filters into the AceDataCloud MCP tools, allowing for more precise query capabilities. Key enhancements include the addition of filters such as `limit`, `tag`, and `private` across various tools, ensuring alignment with backend functionalities. Notably, the `list_apis` tool now efficiently applies server-side filtering, improving performance and accuracy.

items.title: Enhance Group Management with Create and Invite APIs in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new APIs for managing WeChat groups: creating a group and inviting members. The `POST /api/groups` endpoint allows users to initiate a group chat with at least two contacts, while the `POST /api/groups/invite` endpoint enables adding members to an existing group. Both functionalities have been validated through live testing, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Enhance Task Cards with Audio/Video Prompts in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of missing prompts and reference media on Seedance task cards. Key changes include the addition of computed properties for displaying prompts, reference audio, video, and images, ensuring all relevant media is visible. The implementation aligns with existing UI conventions and passes all review checks.
